comment: Mutation-testing kill-rate floors for forge's critical path (Epic U, 1.1.0-alpha.1).
Consumed by .github/workflows/mutmut.yml on its weekly run + on
breaking-change-labeled PRs that touch these modules.
kill_rate_min: fraction of generated mutants that tests MUST catch.
A drop below this floor fails the workflow.
survivors_max: upper bound on surviving mutants per module — a
companion check that flags a regression even when the fraction
still passes due to a growing total count.
pr_gate_modules: flat ``path -> kill_rate_floor`` map for the
scoped, blocking PR-gate (see plan: Mutation-testing PR-gate
scoped subset). Three highest-blast-radius modules only; the
full PR gate ships in 1.4 (Epic U). Touching any path in this
block on a PR triggers a mutmut shard and a blocking floor
check; touching no path skips the gate entirely.
pr_gate_test_map: per-module list of test files the PR-gate mutmut
runner executes for that module. The runner uses these instead of
the whole suite: the default ``pytest`` collects the entire tree
(e2e scaffolds + golden full-generation) under the --cov addopts,
so a *surviving* mutant runs that slow suite to completion and
exceeds the per-shard timeout -> mutmut records every mutant as a
timeout -> total_evaluable==0 -> the gate fails with no signal.
Scoping to each module's own tests keeps a shard at 1-5 min while
still clearing the floors above (measured: provenance ~0.67,
capability_resolver ~0.58, resolver ~0.5x). A module absent from
this map falls back to the default runner.
Update policy mirrors docs/coverage-policy.md's ratchet rules —
tightening floors needs a sprint; loosening them needs a CHANGELOG
entry + RFC-002 justification.
